title: The Anti-Persona - Who NOT to Sell To

questions.question: What is the '80/20 Rule of Misery' in sales?

options.text: 80% of customers are happy, 20% are sad.
80% of your headaches/support tickets come from 20% of your customers (the bad fits).
You should spend 80% of your time on difficult customers to fix them.
80% of sales happen on Fridays.

questions.explanation: Bad fit customers (Anti-Personas) consume disproportionate resources compared to their revenue value.

questions.question: Why is 'Bad Revenue' dangerous for a startup?

options.text: Money is never bad.
It forces you to build features for edge cases that don't scale, distracting you from the main product roadmap.
It makes your bank account look too full.
It requires you to pay more taxes.

questions.explanation: Revenue that comes with custom feature demands or high support costs from the wrong customer type can kill your product focus.

questions.question: How do you identify the 'Education Seeker' (Free Consultant)?

options.text: They ask 'Can I pick your brain?' or 'Just exploring' with no budget/timeline.
They ask for the pricing page immediately.
They are the CEO.
They send you a contract.

questions.explanation: Education seekers want your knowledge but have no intent or ability to buy. They often use phrase like 'just researching'.

questions.question: What is the best way to handle an 'Unfunded Visionary' asking for a discount on Day 1?

options.text: Give them the discount to get the logo.
State your standard price firmly. If they flinch, disqualify them.
Argue with them.
Build a custom plan for them.

questions.explanation: State pricing early to test budget qualification. If they can't afford your base price, they are an Anti-Persona.

questions.question: Which of these is a valid 'Technical Anti-Persona' signal?

options.text: They use a competitor's technology that implies a fundamentally different workflow (e.g. Shopify vs Magento).
They have a website.
They are on LinkedIn.
They use Gmail.

questions.explanation: Incompatible tech stacks are hard disqualifiers. You shouldn't try to sell a Salesforce plugin to a Hubspot shop.

questions.question: Define your Anti-Persona: Describe the type of customer you should actively AVOID. What are the warning signs that someone is not a good fit?
questions.promptForAI: Assess the anti-persona clarity. Check for: (1) specific characteristics to avoid, (2) clear warning signs, (3) reasoning for exclusion. Suggest additional red flags to watch for.
